trans-unit.source: Digitisation
trans-unit.target: Digitisation

trans-unit.source: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__
trans-unit.target: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__

trans-unit.source: Digitisation is the process of converting geographic data from maps or images into digital form commonly represented as vector data. During this procedure, spatial information from maps or images is traced, forming points, polylines, or polygons. To digitize data for a new dataset you always have to start with creating the dataset before filling it with digitized data.
trans-unit.target: Digitisation is the process of converting geographic data from maps or images into digital form commonly represented as vector data. During this procedure, spatial information from maps or images is traced, forming points, polylines, or polygons. To digitize data for a new dataset you always have to start with creating the dataset before filling it with digitized data.

trans-unit.source: The Digitisation Toolbar in QGIS
trans-unit.target: The Digitisation Toolbar in QGIS

trans-unit.source: Digitisation in QGIS is done primarily with the __digitisation toolbar__. To activate the toolbar, navigate to `View` → `Toolbars` → `Digitisation Toolbar`.
trans-unit.target: Digitisation in QGIS is done primarily with the __digitisation toolbar__. To activate the toolbar, navigate to `View` → `Toolbars` → `Digitisation Toolbar`.

trans-unit.source: Creating a new layer
trans-unit.target: Creating a new layer

trans-unit.source: In the top bar, navigate to `Layer` → `Create Layer` → `New GeoPackage Layer` or `New Shapefile Layer`.
trans-unit.target: In the top bar, navigate to `Layer` → `Create Layer` → `New GeoPackage Layer` or `New Shapefile Layer`.

trans-unit.source: A new window will open. Here you can specify the layer properties.
trans-unit.target: A new window will open. Here you can specify the layer properties.

trans-unit.source: Under `Database`, click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to the folder where you want to save the new dataset.
trans-unit.target: Under `Database`, click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to the folder where you want to save the new dataset.

trans-unit.source: *shapefile specific*: Set the file encoding to `UTF-8`.
trans-unit.target: *shapefile specific*: Set the file encoding to `UTF-8`.

trans-unit.source: Select the CRS (Coordinate Reference System) you want to use for the new layer. By default it is set to the project CRS. If you want to change the CRS click on ![](/fig/mIconProjectionEnabled.png).
trans-unit.target: Select the CRS (Coordinate Reference System) you want to use for the new layer. By default it is set to the project CRS. If you want to change the CRS click on ![](/fig/mIconProjectionEnabled.png).

trans-unit.source: Under `New Field`, you can add columns to the new layer. Here you can define what kind of information/data will be available in the attribute table for each feature.
trans-unit.target: Under `New Field`, you can add columns to the new layer. Here you can define what kind of information/data will be available in the attribute table for each feature.

trans-unit.source: `Name`: Give the new column a name representing the information you want to store in it.
trans-unit.target: `Name`: Give the new column a name representing the information you want to store in it.

trans-unit.source: `Type`: Here you can select the data type for the new column. For example, `Text` will add a new column with string (text) data. For numerical data you can choose `Whole number` or `Decimal number`.
trans-unit.target: `Type`: Here you can select the data type for the new column. For example, `Text` will add a new column with string (text) data. For numerical data you can choose `Whole number` or `Decimal number`.

trans-unit.source: `Maximum Length` defines the maximum number of characters the field can have. This is relevant for the length of text or the precision of decimal numbers. For example, you might want to set a high maximum length for text fields if you want long street names such as "Martin Luther King Jr. Boulevard" (34 characters).
trans-unit.target: `Maximum Length` defines the maximum number of characters the field can have. This is relevant for the length of text or the precision of decimal numbers. For example, you might want to set a high maximum length for text fields if you want long street names such as "Martin Luther King Jr. Boulevard" (34 characters).

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionNewAttribute.png) to add the new column to the `Fields List`.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionNewAttribute.png) to add the new column to the `Fields List`.

trans-unit.source: Once you are done adding the fields, click `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done adding the fields, click `OK`.

trans-unit.source: Adding geometries to a layer
trans-unit.target: Adding geometries to a layer

trans-unit.source: Creating point data
trans-unit.target: Creating point data

trans-unit.source: Select the point layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel
trans-unit.target: Select the point layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel

trans-unit.source: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png). Now the layer is in the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png). Now the layer is in the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCapturePoint.png).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCapturePoint.png).

trans-unit.source: Left-click on the feature you want to digitise.
trans-unit.target: Left-click on the feature you want to digitise.

trans-unit.source: Once you click, a window named `[Your Layer Name]- Feature Attribute` will appear. Here you can add the information about this feature to the different columns, based on the attribute table of the layer.
trans-unit.target: Once you click, a window named `[Your Layer Name]- Feature Attribute` will appear. Here you can add the information about this feature to the different columns, based on the attribute table of the layer.

trans-unit.source: ::: % Add another picture with more columns
trans-unit.target: ::: % Add another picture with more columns

trans-unit.source: Once you are done with digitisation ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done with digitisation ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click again on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click again on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Creating line data
trans-unit.target: Creating line data

trans-unit.source: The method is similar to digitising a point (see above). First you have to create a new line layer or use an existing one.
trans-unit.target: The method is similar to digitising a point (see above). First you have to create a new line layer or use an existing one.

trans-unit.source: In the layers panel, select the line layer to which you want to add data.
trans-unit.target: In the layers panel, select the line layer to which you want to add data.

trans-unit.source: In the digitisation toolbar, click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png). Now the layer is in editing mode.
trans-unit.target: In the digitisation toolbar, click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png). Now the layer is in editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCaptureLine.png).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCaptureLine.png).

trans-unit.source: Create the line on the map canvas by <kbd>left-clicking</kbd> to add vertices. When you are done, right-click on the last point to finish editing the geometry.
trans-unit.target: Create the line on the map canvas by <kbd>left-clicking</kbd> to add vertices. When you are done, right-click on the last point to finish editing the geometry.

trans-unit.source: A new window titled `[Your Layer Name]- Feature Attribute` will appear. Here you can fill in the column information for the feature.
trans-unit.target: A new window titled `[Your Layer Name]- Feature Attribute` will appear. Here you can fill in the column information for the feature.

trans-unit.source: Once you are done with digitisation, click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done with digitisation, click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click on the ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) icon again to end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on the ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) icon again to end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Creating polygon data
trans-unit.target: Creating polygon data

trans-unit.source: In the layers panel, select the polygon layer to which you want to add data.
trans-unit.target: In the layers panel, select the polygon layer to which you want to add data.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to start the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to start the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Click on `Capture Polygon`![](/fig/mActionCapturePolygon.png) to add polygons.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Capture Polygon`![](/fig/mActionCapturePolygon.png) to add polygons.

trans-unit.source: Draw a polygon in the map canvas using <kbd>Left-click</kbd>. <kbd>Right-click</kbd> will finish the polygon creation and join the first and the last point you have added.
trans-unit.target: Draw a polygon in the map canvas using <kbd>Left-click</kbd>. <kbd>Right-click</kbd> will finish the polygon creation and join the first and the last point you have added.

trans-unit.source: A new window will open. Here you can add the column information for this feature.
trans-unit.target: A new window will open. Here you can add the column information for this feature.

trans-unit.source: Save edits by clicking on the ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) icon and exiting the edit mode by clicking on the ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) icon.
trans-unit.target: Save edits by clicking on the ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) icon and exiting the edit mode by clicking on the ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) icon.

trans-unit.source: Modifying existing geometries
trans-unit.target: Modifying existing geometries

trans-unit.source: In the layers panel, select the layer with the geometry you want to edit by clicking on it. It will appear blue.
trans-unit.target: In the layers panel, select the layer with the geometry you want to edit by clicking on it. It will appear blue.

trans-unit.source: In the digitising toolbar, click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) start `edit mode`.
trans-unit.target: In the digitising toolbar, click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) start `edit mode`.

trans-unit.source: In the digitising toolbar, click on ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_vertex_tool.png). Now, you can move and edit vertices of geometries.
trans-unit.target: In the digitising toolbar, click on ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_vertex_tool.png). Now, you can move and edit vertices of geometries.

trans-unit.source: Once you are done, don't forget to exit the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) and saving your edits.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done, don't forget to exit the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) and saving your edits.

trans-unit.source: Adding Ring to existing polygon layer
trans-unit.target: Adding Ring to existing polygon layer

trans-unit.source: In QGIS, adding rings to polygons is done with the "Advanced Digitisation Toolbar". To activate the toolbar, navigate to `View` → `Toolbars` → `Advanced Digitisation Toolbar`.
trans-unit.target: In QGIS, adding rings to polygons is done with the "Advanced Digitisation Toolbar". To activate the toolbar, navigate to `View` → `Toolbars` → `Advanced Digitisation Toolbar`.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to start the editing mode of a polygon layer.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to start the editing mode of a polygon layer.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionAddRing.png) (e.g. map the inner courtyard of a building, or - as shown in the video - create a circle to mark an isle in the lake).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionAddRing.png) (e.g. map the inner courtyard of a building, or - as shown in the video - create a circle to mark an isle in the lake).

trans-unit.source: Further resources
trans-unit.target: Further resources

trans-unit.source: The YouTube Video below shows the whole process of digitizing polygons in QGIS in some more detail. Note that the YouTuber is using an older version of QGIS, so things might be different in your version.
trans-unit.target: The YouTube Video below shows the whole process of digitizing polygons in QGIS in some more detail. Note that the YouTuber is using an older version of QGIS, so things might be different in your version.

trans-unit.source: YouTube video player
trans-unit.target: YouTube video player
